expected_signals.summary: Ollama raised a $65 million Series B led by Theory Ventures, bringing total funding to $88 million.
Morgan said Ollama is used by over 8.9 million developers every month and is present in 85% of the Fortune 500.
expected_signals.anchor_quote: used by over 8.9 million developers every month

expected_signals.summary: Ollama monetizes access to larger hosted models through neocloud subscription tiers and GPU-time pricing.
expected_signals.anchor_quote: hosts on its neocloud via several subscription tiers

expected_signals.summary: Morgan said larger open models becoming useful for agentic coding work around January was a business proving point for Ollama.
expected_signals.anchor_quote: the proving point for Ollama as a business happened around January

expected_signals.summary: Some users criticized Ollama's push into cloud services as attention shifting away from the free project.
expected_signals.anchor_quote: drawing attention away from its beloved free project

expected_signals.summary: Ollama and its board member said the cloud business does not change the free desktop product's core premise.
expected_signals.anchor_quote: Nothing has changed for the core product that's free on the desktop

notes: Core subject is Ollama as company/tool. Manual proof: each anchor_quote was cross-checked as a verbatim substring in the provided text; this extraction-only task has no separate runnable verifier.
